As the interview got underway, Sarah felt a flutter in her stomach that felt like a live wire. She tried to focus on the task at hand, but her mind kept wandering to the endless anxiety that had been gnawing at her all week. She took deep breaths and tried to calm herself, but it was no use. Every time her palms got sweaty or her heartbeat raced, she could feel her anxiety rising.

As soon as Dr. Kaye finished delivering the news, I felt a sudden wave of anxiety wash over me. I had just been diagnosed with a chronic illness that required daily medication and strict dietary restrictions. The doctor had explained that I had a genetic mutation that increased my risk of developing cancer, but the news still felt like a gut punch.
My hands shook as I tried to steady myself, and I could feel the sweat forming on my forehead. I took a deep breath and tried to focus on the present moment. I needed to stay calm and take it one day at a time.

As I sit down to process the latest medical news, my heart pounds in my chest. It's been months since my doctor last checked in with me, and now I have a new diagnosis: cancer.
